    echo "AIDE baseline database is outdated. Updating now..."

if ! command -v aide &> /dev/null; then

CONFIG_FILE="/etc/aide.conf"
if [ ! -f $CONFIG_FILE ]; then
    echo "Creating AIDE configuration file..."

fi

#!/bin/bash
# Check if postfix is installed, if not install it
if ! rpm -q --quiet postfix; then
    yum install -y postfix
fi

# Start and enable postfix service
systemctl start postfix
systemctl enable postfix

# Install AIDE if not already installed
if ! rpm -q --quiet aide; then
    yum install -y aide
fi

# Initialize AIDE database if it doesn't exist
if [ ! -f /var/lib/aide/aide.db.gz ]; then
    aide --init
    mv /tmp/aide.db.new.gz /var/lib/aide/aide.db.gz
fi

# Create cron job to run AIDE check and email results
echo "0 5 * * * root aide --check | mail -s 'AIDE Integrity Check Report' root" > /etc/cron.d/aide_email
```

# This script will enable FIPS mode on RHEL 8 system

# Check if the system is running RHEL 8
if grep -q "Red Hat Enterprise Linux release 8" /etc/redhat-release; then
    echo "This system is running RHEL 8."
else
    echo "This script is for RHEL 8 systems only. Exiting..."
    exit 1
fi

if grep -q "fips=1" /proc/sys/crypto/fips_enabled; then
    echo "FIPS mode is already enabled."

    # Enable FIPS mode in GRUB configuration
    grubby --update-kernel=ALL --args="fips=1"
    
    # Check if the previous command was successful
    if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
        echo "FIPS mode has been enabled."
        
        # Reboot the system to apply changes
        read -p "Do you want to reboot now to apply changes? (yes/no): " choice
        case "$choice" in 
          yes ) echo "Rebooting..."; reboot;;
          no ) echo "Please reboot manually for changes to take effect."; exit 0;;
          * ) echo "Invalid response. Please type 'yes' or 'no'."; exit 1;;
        esac
    else
        echo "Failed to enable FIPS mode. Exiting..."
        exit 1
    fi
fi

# This script will enable FIPS mode on RHEL 8 system

    echo "This system is running RHEL 8."
else
    echo "This script is for RHEL 8 systems only. Exiting..."
    exit 1
fi

# Check if FIPS mode is already enabled
if grep -q "fips=1" /proc/sys/crypto/fips_enabled; then
    echo "FIPS mode is already enabled."
else
    # Enable FIPS mode in GRUB configuration
    grubby --update-kernel=ALL --args="fips=1"
    
    # Check if the previous command was successful
    if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
        echo "FIPS mode has been enabled."
        
        # Reboot the system to apply changes
        read -p "Do you want to reboot now to apply changes? (yes/no): " choice

# This script sets the system-wide cryptographic policy to default-strength profile

# Define the desired policy

# Check if the current policy is not the desired one
if [ "$CURRENT_POLICY" != "$DESIRED_POLICY" ]; then

#!/bin/bash
# This script is idempotent and will ensure GnuTLS configuration to honor system crypto policy 
# and does not permit legacy/insecure protocol versions (SSL 3.0, TLS 1.0, TLS 1.1, DTLS 0.9, DTLS 1.0)

GNUTLS_CONFIG="/etc/gnutls/config"
BACKUP_FILE="/etc/gnutls/config.bak"

# Backup the original config file
if [ ! -f "${BACKUP_FILE}" ]; then
    cp ${GNUTLS_CONFIG} ${BACKUP_FILE}
fi

# Ensure system-wide cryptographic policy is deferred to
sed -i 's/^#\? *override-mode.*$/override-mode = allowlist/' ${GNUTLS_CONFIG}

# Disable insecure protocol versions
for version in ssl3 tls1.0 tls1.1 dtls0.9 dtls1.0; do
    if grep -q "^disabled-protocol" ${GNUTLS_CONFIG}; then
        sed -i "/^disabled-protocols.*$/ s/$/, $version/" ${GNUTLS_CONFIG}
    else
        echo "disabled-protocols = $version" >> ${GNUTLS_CONFIG}
    fi
done

#!/bin/bash
# This script is idempotent and will ensure that only FIPS 140-2 validated ciphers are used for SSH server

# Define the list of FIPS 140-2 validated ciphers in order of strength (strongest to weakest)
fips_ciphers=(aes256-ctr aes192-ctr aes128-ctr aes256-cbc aes192-cbc aes128-cbc)

# Define the path to the SSH server configuration file
sshd_config="/etc/ssh/sshd_config"

# Check if the SSH server configuration file exists
if [ -f "$sshd_config" ]; then
    # Backup the original SSH server configuration file
    cp $sshd_config{,.bak}
    
    # Uncomment and set Ciphers line in the SSH server configuration file to use only FIPS 140-2 validated ciphers
    sed -i '/^Ciphers/s/^#//' $sshd_config
    sed -i "/^Ciphers/s/=.*/= ${fips_ciphers[*]}/" $sshd_config
    
    # Restart the SSH server to apply changes
    systemctl restart sshd
else
    echo "SSH server configuration file not found at $sshd_config."
fi

#!/bin/bash
# This script sets up an automatic screensaver timeout of 15 minutes for GNOME desktop environment on RHEL 8.

# Define the settings file path
settings_file="/etc/dconf/db/local.d/00-screensaver"

# Create a backup of the original file if it doesn't exist
if [ ! -f "${settings_file}.bak" ]; then
    sudo cp "$settings_file" "${settings_file}.bak"
fi

# Define the settings to be added or updated
settings=(
    "/org/gnome/desktop/session/idle-delay"
    "/org/gnome/settings-daemon/plugins/power/sleep-inactive-ac-timeout"
    "/org/gnome/settings-daemon/plugins/power/sleep-inactive-battery-timeout"
)

# Define the values to be set for each setting
values=(15 600 600)

# Create a new dconf profile if it doesn't exist
if [ ! -f "$settings_file" ]; then
    echo "[org/gnome/desktop/screensaver]" | sudo tee "$settings_file" > /dev/null

#!/bin/bash
# This script sets up an automatic screensaver timeout of 15 minutes for GNOME desktop environment on RHEL 8.

# Define the settings file path
settings_file="/etc/dconf/db/local.d/00-screensaver"

# Create a backup of the original file if it doesn't exist
if [ ! -f "${settings_file}.bak" ]; then
    sudo cp "$settings_file" "${settings_file}.bak"
fi

# Define the settings to be added or updated
settings=(
    "/org/gnome/desktop/session/idle-delay"
    "/org/gnome/settings-daemon/plugins/power/sleep-inactive-ac-timeout"
    "/org/gnome/settings-daemon/plugins/power/sleep-inactive-battery-timeout"
)

# Define the values to be set for each setting
values=(15 600 600)

# Create a new dconf profile if it doesn't exist
if [ ! -f "$settings_file" ]; then
    echo "[org/gnome/desktop/screensaver]" | sudo tee "$settings_file" > /dev/null
fi

# Add or update the settings in the dconf profile
for ((i=0; i<${#settings[@]}; i++)); do
    setting=${settings[$i]}
    value=${values[$i]}
    if grep -q "^$setting" "$settings_file"; then
        sudo sed -i "/^$setting/c $setting"="'$value'" "$settings_file"
    else
        echo "$setting='$value'" | sudo tee -a "$settings_file" > /dev/null
    fi
done

# Update the dconf database with the new settings
sudo dconf update

# Lock the dconf profile to prevent individual users from overriding it
sudo chattr +i "$settings_file"
```

backup_file="/etc/pam.d/password-auth.bak"

#!/bin/bash
# This script configures PAM to lock out accounts after failed login attempts

# Define the configuration file path
config_file="/etc/pam.d/password-auth"
backup_file="/etc/pam.d/password-auth.bak"

# Backup the original file
cp $config_file $backup_file

# Check if the pam_tally2 module is already present in the configuration file
if grep -q "^auth.*pam_tally2.so" $config_file; then
    echo "PAM tally2 module already configured."
else
    # Add the pam_tally2 module to the auth section of the configuration file
    sed -i '/^auth.*pam_securetty.so/a auth        required      pam_tally2.so onerr=fail audit silent deny=5 unlock_time=900' $config_file
fi

# Check if the pam_faillock module is already present in the configuration file
if grep -q "^auth.*pam_faillock.so" $config_file; then
    echo "PAM faillock module already configured."
else
    # Add the pam_faillock module to the auth section of the configuration file
    sed -i '/^auth.*pam_tally2.so/a auth        required      pam_faillock.so preauth silent audit deny=5 unlock_time=900' $config_file
fi

# Check if the pam_unix module is already present in the configuration file
if grep -q "^account.*pam_unix.so" $config_file; then
    echo "PAM unix module already configured."
else
    # Add the pam_unix module to the account section of the configuration file
    sed -i '/^account.*pam_tally2.so/a account     required      pam_unix.so' $config_file
fi
```

        mv ${pam_file}.bak $pam_file
    fi
else

fi

# Check if pwquality is installed, install it if not

    # Add maxrepeat parameter to the config file if it doesn't exist

echo "[Service]

echo "[Login]

IdleActionSec=$IDLE_TIMEOUT" > /etc/systemd/logind.conf.d/idle-timeout.conf

systemctl daemon-reload
systemctl restart systemd-logind

current_setting=$(grep -E '^PASS_MAX_DAYS' /etc/login.defs | awk '{print $2}')

    sed -i "/^PASS_MAX_DAYS/ c\PASS_MAX_DAYS $max_lifetime" /etc/login.defs

  # Check if password age is less than 1 day

#!/bin/bash
# This script will ensure every interactive user's local initialization files are no more permissive than owner read/write/execute plus group read only (octal 0740)

# Get a list of all human users with home directories and the root account
users=$(getent passwd | awk -F: '$3 >= 1000 && $6 != "" {print $1}')

for user in $users; do
  # Get the home directory for this user
  homedir=$(getent passwd "$user" | cut -d: -f6)
  
  # Loop over all files and directories in the home directory
  find "$homedir" -maxdepth 1 \( ! -name . \) -type d,f | while read file; do
    # Get the current permissions for this file or directory
    perms=$(stat -c %a "$file")
    
    # If the permissions are more permissive than required, tighten them
    if [ $perms -gt 740 ]; then

done

users=$(getent passwd | awk -F: '$3 >= 1000 && $6 != "" {print $1}')

for user in $users; do
  # Get the home directory for this user
  homedir=$(getent passwd "$user" | cut -d: -f6)
  
  # Loop over all files and directories in the home directory

current_umask=$(bash -c 'echo $-' | grep -q "u="; echo $?)

# This script disables the legacy virtual system-call mechanism on RHEL 8 systems.

# Define the configuration file path
config_file="/boot/grub2/grubenv"

# Check if the config file exists
if [ -f "$config_file" ]; then
    # Check if the kernel parameter is already set
    if grep -q '^legacy_syscalls=0' $config_file; then
        echo "Legacy syscall mechanism is already disabled."
    else
        # Append the kernel parameter to the config file
        echo 'legacy_syscalls=0' >> $config_file
        
        # Update grub configuration
        grub2-mkconfig -o /boot/grub2/grub.cfg

else

fi

REMOTE_PORT="514"

#!/bin/bash
# This script configures the IPv4 networking stack to ignore ICMP echo requests sent to broadcast or multicast addresses.

# Define the sysctl settings
SYSCTL_SETTINGS=(
    "net.ipv4.icmp_echo_ignore_broadcasts = 1"
)

# Function to set sysctl settings
function set_sysctl {
    local key=$1
    local value=$2
    local config_file="/etc/sysctl.conf"
    
    # Check if the setting is already present in the configuration file
    if grep -q "^$key = $value" $config_file; then
        echo "Sysctl setting '$key' already exists with the correct value."
    else
        # If not, append it to the configuration file
        echo "$key = $value" >> $config_file
        echo "Added sysctl setting '$key' with value '$value' to $config_file."
        
        # Load the new settings immediately
        sysctl -w "$key=$value" > /dev/null
    fi
}

# Apply the sysctl settings
for setting in "${SYSCTL_SETTINGS[@]}"; do
    set_sysctl $(echo $setting | tr '=' ' ')
done

#!/bin/bash

    # Check if the boot partition is already set to no suid/sgid

        # If not, add nosuid and noexec to the options
        new_options=$(echo "$mount_options,nosuid,noexec")
        sed -i "s|\(.*\) /boot \(.*\)|\1 /boot \2 $new_options|" /etc/fstab

    fi
fi

# This script will restrict read access to the kernel message buffer so that only privileged users may view it.

if [ -f "$file_path" ]; then
    # Read the current value of dmesg_restrict
    current_value=$(cat $file_path)
    
    # If the current value is not 1, set it to 1 and apply immediately
    if [ "$current_value" != "1" ]; then
        echo "1" > $file_path
        
        # Check if the change was applied successfully
        new_value=$(cat $file_path)
        if [ "$new_value" == "1" ]; then
            echo "Successfully set dmesg_restrict to 1 and applied immediately."
            
            # Create a backup of /etc/sysctl.conf
            cp /etc/sysctl.conf /etc/sysctl.conf.bak
            
            # Append the setting to /etc/sysctl.conf so it persists across reboots
            echo "kernel.dmesg_restrict = 1" >> /etc/sysctl.conf
        else
            echo "Failed to set dmesg_restrict to 1."
        fi
    else
        echo "dmesg_restrict is already set to 1."
    fi
else
    echo "The file $file_path does not exist."
fi

# This script disables the kernel crash-dump analyzer service on RHEL 8 systems.

    # Stop and disable the kernel crash-dump analyzer service
    systemctl stop kdump.service
    systemctl disable kdump.service

    if [[ -f /etc/systemd/system/kdump.service ]]; then
        rm -f /etc/systemd/system/kdump.service

    if [[ $(grep -E '^ExecStart=' /etc/systemd/system.conf) ]]; then
        sed -i '/^ExecStart=/d' /etc/systemd/system.conf
    fi
    
    echo "Kernel crash-dump analyzer service has been disabled and prevented from starting."
else

#!/bin/bash
# Check if SELinux is installed
if ! command -v sestatus &> /dev/null; then
    echo "SELinux is not installed. Please install it and try again."
    exit 1
fi

# Check if the current mode of SELinux is enforcing
enforce=$(sestatus | awk '/Current mode:/ {print $3}')
if [ "$enforce" != "enforcing" ]; then
    echo "SELinux is not in enforcing mode. Please set it to enforcing and try again."
    exit 1
fi

# Check if the SELinux boolean 'deny_execmem' is enabled
status=$(getsebool deny_execmem | awk -F'--> ' '{print $2}')
if [ "$status" != "on" ]; then
    echo "The SELinux boolean 'deny_execmem' is not enabled. Enabling it now..."
    setsebool -P deny_execmem on
fi

# Check if the SELinux boolean 'global_ssp' is disabled
status=$(getsebool global_ssp | awk -F'--> ' '{print $2}')
if [ "$status" != "off" ]; then
    echo "The SELinux boolean 'global_ssp' is not disabled. Disabling it now..."
    setsebool -P global_ssp off
fi

echo "SELinux application allowlisting (file-access policy) subsystem has been configured to operate under a deny-all, permit-by-exception model."

#!/bin/bash
# Check if SELinux is installed
if ! command -v sestatus &> /dev/null; then
    echo "SELinux is not installed. Please install it and try again."
    exit 1
fi

# Check if the current mode of SELinux is enforcing
enforce=$(sestatus | awk '/Current mode:/ {print $3}')
if [ "$enforce" != "enforcing" ]; then
    echo "SELinux is not in enforcing mode. Please set it to enforcing and try again."
    exit 1
fi

# Check if the SELinux boolean 'deny_execmem' is enabled
status=$(getsebool deny_execmem | awk -F'--> ' '{print $2}')
if [ "$status" != "on" ]; then
    echo "The SELinux boolean 'deny_execmem' is not enabled. Enabling it now..."
    setsebool -P deny_execmem on
fi

# Check if the SELinux boolean 'global_ssp' is disabled
status=$(getsebool global_ssp | awk -F'--> ' '{print $2}')
if [ "$status" != "off" ]; then
    echo "The SELinux boolean 'global_ssp' is not disabled. Disabling it now..."
    setsebool -P global_ssp off
fi

echo "SELinux application allowlisting (file-access policy) subsystem has been configured to operate under a deny-all, permit-by-exception model."

default_poll_interval=32

    # Loop through each server line in the config file

#!/bin/bash
# This script configures SSH server to not trust per-user cache of previously seen remote host public keys

SSH_CONFIG="/etc/ssh/sshd_config"
BACKUP_FILE="/etc/ssh/sshd_config.bak"

if [ -f "$SSH_CONFIG" ]; then
    # Backup the original sshd_config file
    cp $SSH_CONFIG $BACKUP_FILE
    
    # Check if HostbasedAuthentication is already set to no in the config file
    if grep -q '^HostbasedAuthentication no' $SSH_CONFIG; then
        echo "HostbasedAuthentication is already set to no. No changes needed."

        # If not, append the line to disable host-based authentication
        echo "HostbasedAuthentication no" >> $SSH_CONFIG

        systemctl restart sshd

else
    echo "$SSH_CONFIG not found. Please check if the SSH server is installed correctly."
fi

#!/bin/bash
# This script is idempotent and will ensure SSH server checks ownership and permissions of user's home directory and SSH-related files.

# Define the function to check and fix permissions
check_and_fix() {
    local file="$1"
    if [ -f "$file" ] || [ -d "$file" ]; then
        # Check for world writable permission
        if [ "$(stat -c %a "$file")" -gt 644 ]; then
            chmod o-w "$file"
        fi
        # Check for other write permission
        if [ "$(stat -c %A "$file" | cut -c9)" = "w" ]; then
            chmod o-w "$file"
        fi
    else
        echo "File $file does not exist."
    fi
}

# Check and fix permissions for SSH related files
check_and_fix /etc/ssh/sshd_config
check_and_fix ~/.ssh
check_and_fix ~/.ssh/authorized_keys

# Restart sshd service to apply changes
systemctl restart sshd

#!/bin/bash
# This script generates an initial device-authorization policy reflecting the USB devices currently connected to the system

# Get list of current USB devices
usb_devices=$(lsusb)

# Create a new policy file
policy_file="/etc/usbguard/rules.conf"
touch $policy_file
chmod 600 $policy_file

# Add each device to the policy file
while read -r line; do
    if [[ $line == *": ID "* ]]; then
        id=$(echo $line | awk '{print $6}')
        echo "allow with-interface match-all { $id }" >> $policy_file
    fi
done <<< "$usb_devices"

# Apply the policy
systemctl restart usbguard

    echo "This system is currently running in a graphical desktop mode."
else
    # Check if X Windows components are installed
    xorg_packages=$(rpm -qa | grep '^xorg-' | tr '\n' ' ')

        echo "Removing X Windows graphical display server components..."

        # Check if removal was successful
        if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
            echo "X Windows graphical display server components removed successfully."
        else
            echo "Failed to remove X Windows graphical display server components. Please check the system logs for more information."

    else
        echo "No X Windows graphical display server components found on this system."
    fi
fi

#!/bin/bash
# This script sets the system's default boot state to multi-user, non-graphical mode

# Define the target file and its content
target_file="/etc/systemd/system/default.target"
target_content="Multi-User System"

if grep -q "$target_content" $target_file; then
    echo "Default boot state is already set to multi-user, non-graphical mode."
else
    # If not, change it and persist across reboots
    echo "Changing default boot state to multi-user, non-graphical mode..."
    ln -sf /lib/systemd/system/multi-user.target $target_file
    systemctl set-default multi-user.target
fi

#!/bin/bash
# This script sets the boot-time audit event queue to at least 8192 entries

# Define the desired value for the audit_backlog_limit parameter
desired_value="8192"

# Check if the current value of the audit_backlog_limit parameter is less than the desired value
current_value=$(grep "^\s*linux" /boot/grub2/grub.cfg | grep -oP 'audit_backlog_limit=\K[0-9]+')
if [ -z "$current_value" ] || [ "$current_value" -lt "$desired_value" ]; then
  # If the current value is less than the desired value, or if it doesn't exist, update grub.cfg and grub.conf
  sed -i 's/\(linux[^"]*\)"\?audit_backlog_limit=[0-9]*"/\1 audit_backlog_limit='"$desired_value"'"/g' /boot/grub2/grub.cfg
  sed -i 's/\(linux[^"]*\)"\?audit_backlog_limit=[0-9]*"/\1 audit_backlog_limit='"$desired_value"'"/g' /etc/default/grub
  
  # Regenerate the grub configuration file
  grub2-mkconfig -o /boot/grub2/grub.cfg
fi

#!/bin/bash
# This script makes the audit configuration immutable on RHEL 8 system

# Define the file to be locked and its backup location
file="/etc/audit/rules.d/audit.rules"
backup_dir="/var/lib/audit"
backup_file="${backup_dir}/audit.rules.bak"

# Check if the file exists
if [ -f "$file" ]; then
    # Create backup directory if it doesn't exist
    mkdir -p $backup_dir
    
    # Backup the original file
    cp $file $backup_file
    
    # Lock the file to prevent changes
    chattr +i $file
else
    echo "File not found: $file"
fi

AUDIT_RULES="-a always,exit -F arch=b32 -S fchown -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=-1 -k perm_mod"
AUDIT_RULES+=" -a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S fchown -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=-1 -k perm_mod"

# Check if audit rule already exists
if ! (auditctl -l | grep -q "$AUDIT_RULES"); then
    # If not, add the rule
    echo "$AUDIT_RULES" > /etc/audit/rules.d/99-perm_mod.rules
    
    # Reload audit rules to include new rule
    service auditd reload
fi
```

RULE64="-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S fchown -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=4294967295 -k perm_mod"

if ! grep -q -- "$RULE64" /etc/audit/rules.d/audit.rules; then
    # If it doesn't exist, append it to the end of the file

fi

# Restart the audit daemon to apply changes
systemctl restart auditd

'-a always,exit -F arch=b32 -S open -F exit=-EACCES -F auid>=1000 --identify="access"'
'-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S open -F exit=-EACCES -F auid>=1000 --identify="access"'
'-a always,exit -F arch=b32 -S open -F exit=-EPERM -F auid>=1000 --identify="access"'
'-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S open -F exit=-EPERM -F auid>=1000 --identify="access"'
)

# Check if the rules already exist in the auditd configuration file
for RULE in "${RULES[@]}"; do
    if ! grep -q -- "$RULE" /etc/audit/rules.d/*; then
        echo "Adding rule: $RULE"
        echo "-a always,exit -F arch=b32 -S open -F exit=-EACCES -F auid>=1000 --identify=\"access\"" >> /etc/audit/rules.d/audit.rules
        echo "-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S open -F exit=-EACCES -F auid>=1000 --identify=\"access\"" >> /etc/audit/rules.d/audit.rules
        echo "-a always,exit -F arch=b32 -S open -F exit=-EPERM -F auid>=1000 --identify=\"access\"" >> /etc/audit/rules.d/audit.rules
        echo "-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S open -F exit=-EPERM -F auid>=1000 --identify=\"access\"" >> /etc/audit/rules.d/audit.rules
    fi
done

# Restart the audit daemon to load new rules
sudo systemctl restart auditd

#!/bin/bash
# This script configures the system audit daemon to respond by switching the system into single-user mode when it encounters a disk error while writing audit records.

# Define the configuration file path
config_file="/etc/audit/auditd.conf"

# Check if the configuration file exists
if [ -f "$config_file" ]; then
    # Check if the 'disk_error_action' option is already set to 'single' in the configuration file
    if grep -qP '^disk_error_action\s*=\s*single' $config_file; then
        echo "The system audit daemon is already configured to switch into single-user mode when encountering a disk error."
    else
        # If the option is not set, append it to the configuration file
        echo 'disk_error_action = single' >> $config_file
        echo "The system audit daemon has been configured to switch into single-user mode when encountering a disk error."
    fi
else
    echo "The auditd.conf file does not exist, please check the configuration of your system audit daemon."
fi

#!/bin/bash
# Check if auditd is installed
if rpm -q audit &> /dev/null; then
    # Define the config file path
    AUDITD_CONF="/etc/audit/auditd.conf"
    
    # Check if the config file exists
    if [ -f "$AUDITD_CONF" ]; then
        # Check if the disk_full_action is already set to SINGLE
        if grep -qP '^disk_full_action\s*=\s*SINGLE' $AUDITD_CONF; then
            echo "Disk full action is already set to single-user mode."
        else
            # Backup the original config file
            cp $AUDITD_CONF ${AUDITD_CONF}.bak
            
            # Set disk_full_action to SINGLE and append it to the config file
            echo "disk_full_action = SINGLE" >> $AUDITD_CONF
            
            echo "Disk full action has been set to single-user mode."
        fi
    else
        # If the config file does not exist, create a new one and set disk_full_action to SINGLE
        echo "disk_full_action = SINGLE" > $AUDITD_CONF
        
        echo "Disk full action has been set to single-user mode."
    fi
else
    # If auditd is not installed, install it and set disk_full_action to SINGLE
    yum -y install audit
    
    AUDITD_CONF="/etc/audit/auditd.conf"
    echo "disk_full_action = SINGLE" > $AUDITD_CONF
    
    echo "Disk full action has been set to single-user mode."
fi

#!/bin/bash
# This script ensures that every audit rule definition file used by the audit subsystem permits access only to its owner (read and write, with no permissions for group or others)

# Find all audit rule files
files=$(find /etc/audit -type f)

for file in $files; do
    # Check if the file has mode 0600
    if [[ "$(stat -c %a "$file")" != "600" ]]; then
        # If not, change its permissions to 0600
        chmod 0600 "$file"
    fi
done

#!/bin/bash
# This script ensures that every audit rule definition file used by the audit subsystem permits access only to its owner (read and write, with no permissions for group or others)

# Find all audit rule files
files=$(find /etc/audit -type f)

for file in $files; do
    # Check if the file has mode 0600
    if [[ "$(stat -c %a "$file")" != "600" ]]; then
        # If not, change its permissions to 0600
        chmod 0600 "$file"
    fi
done
